LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF CHRISTIAN W. FREY AKA CHRISTIAN FREY. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THE $1922.68 IS PAID EARNINGS AND THAT 75% OF THAT SUM IS EXEMPT PER CCP 704.070(B)(2). THEREFORE, THE COURT ORDERS THE SHERIFF TO RELEASE $1,442.01 BACK TO DEFENDANT FREY. IT THE TENTATIVE RULING IS NOT CONTESTED, THE COURT WILL EXECUTE A JUDICIAL COUNCIL ORDER THAT ADOPTS THE RULING. THE COURT WILL PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Nov-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Christian Frey, 415-794-8033 Defendant in Pro Per.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Defendant informs the Court Plaintiff submits on the papers. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF CHRISTIAN W. FREY AKA CHRISTIAN FREY.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The claim of exemption is GRANTED IN PART. The court determines that the $1922.68 is paid earnings and that 75% of that sum is exempt per CCP 704.070(b)(2). Therefore, the court orders the sheriff to release $1,442.01 back to defendant Frey. It the tentative ruling is not contested, the court will execute a judicial council order that adopts the ruling.

The Court will prepare an order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
